title: monardella viridis subsp. viridis
intro: Monardella viridis subsp. viridis, also known as Green Mountain Monardella, is a perennial herb native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. This plant is a member of the mint family (Lamiaceae) and is prized for its fragrant, greenish-yellow flowers and medicinal properties. The leaves and flowers of Monardella viridis subsp. viridis have been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, sore throats, and respiratory problems, due to their antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. Monardella viridis subsp. viridis can be prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or infusion, and is also used in aromatherapy to promote relaxation and relieve stress.
